description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, ornate brass horn featuring decorative gold patterns. The base is a rectangular, dark wooden box with a flat top surface supporting a circular black turntable platter and a visible vinyl record. A metallic tonearm with a rounded head rests on the record, and a hand-crank protrudes from the side, suggesting manual operation. The horn is attached to the base by a curved support and is the most visually striking element, flaring outward with concentric gold lines and floral motifs. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century phonographs, combining wood, metal, and vinyl elements. The object exudes a sense of nostalgia and craftsmanship, making it a decorative as well as functional piece.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large, ornate brass horn, dark wooden base, and visible vinyl record. The design features decorative gold patterns and a hand-crank for manual operation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved brass horn, tonearm resting on the record, and the hand-crank on the left side of the wooden base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the flared horn and the hand-crank extending horizontally from the left side.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the horn's curve and the tonearm visible, but the hand-crank is not in view.
description_view_3: Front view directly facing the ornate opening of the horn, with the record and tonearm clearly visible on the base.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and circular, the turntable platter is black and occupies the central top surface of the wooden base. It is slightly raised, with a smooth, glossy finish and a visible vinyl record placed on top. The platter appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with no visible grooves or additional markings beyond the record itself.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curved, extending from the right side of the base toward the center of the platter. It is medium in length, with a rounded head positioned over the record. The finish is dark, likely steel or painted metal, and it appears to be resting on the record.
